tasks.title: Types, constants, and file I/O
tasks.description: WHAT: Define the core TypeScript types matching the PRD schema and implement the foundational file operations — discoverTaskFiles (scan plans/ for tasks.json files) and readTasksFile (parse JSON with null-vs-throw error handling). Also define the named constants (SCHEMA_VERSION, TASKS_FILENAME, MAX_NESTING_DEPTH, etc.) that all subsequent tasks reference.
tasks.design: HOW: Create src/lib/tasks.ts with interfaces: Epic {id, title, created}, Task {id, title, status: TaskStatus, priority, labels, description, design, acceptance, notes, dependencies, comments: Comment[], closeReason}, Comment {message, timestamp}, TasksFile {version, epics: Epic[], tasks: Task[]}, TaskStatus = 'open' | 'in_progress' | 'closed', EpicInfo {epics: Array<{id, title}>, primaryEpicId, totalTasks, closedTasks, openTasks, inProgressTasks, allClosed}, ReadyTask {id, title, priority, labels}. discoverTaskFiles(cwd?): string[] uses readdirSync on plans/ dir matching the readPlans pattern in plans.ts. readTasksFile(filePath): TasksFile | null returns null on missing, throws on corrupt JSON. Add 'test': 'bun test' to package.json.
acceptance: All types defined matching PRD schema
Named constants defined: SCHEMA_VERSION=1, TASKS_FILENAME='tasks.json', MAX_NESTING_DEPTH=3 (lock constants live in lock.ts, created in FORGE-1.11)
discoverTaskFiles scans plans/ subdirs + plans/tasks.json root
readTasksFile returns null for missing file
readTasksFile throws descriptive error for corrupt JSON
package.json has 'test': 'bun test' script
Unit tests pass for discoverTaskFiles and readTasksFile

tasks.title: Query functions: queryFeatureTasks and getReadyTasks
tasks.description: WHAT: Implement the two core query functions that replace beads.ts queryBeadsEpic and getReadyTasks. queryFeatureTasks aggregates task stats across all epics in a feature's tasks.json into an EpicInfo. getReadyTasks scans all task files and returns unblocked leaf tasks. Both are synchronous (matching readFileSync pattern in plans.ts).
tasks.design: HOW: queryFeatureTasks(feature, cwd?): EpicInfo | null — reads plans/<feature>/tasks.json via readTasksFile, counts task statuses across all epics, returns null when file missing or has no tasks. allClosed = totalTasks > 0 && closedTasks === totalTasks && every epic has >=1 task. getReadyTasks(cwd?, feature?): ReadyTask[] — when feature provided, reads only that file but loads all files for dep status lookup; when omitted scans all. Ready logic: collect all tasks, build children set (any ID that is prefix+'.' of another), filter to leaves, filter to status=open, check all deps are closed or in_progress.
acceptance: queryFeatureTasks returns correct EpicInfo with aggregated counts
queryFeatureTasks returns null for missing tasks.json
queryFeatureTasks returns null for empty scaffold (no tasks)
Epic with 0 tasks not considered closed for allClosed
getReadyTasks returns leaf tasks only (containers excluded)
getReadyTasks excludes tasks with open deps
getReadyTasks includes tasks with in_progress deps
getReadyTasks(cwd, feature) scopes to feature but resolves cross-file deps
Both functions are synchronous (not Promise-returning)
Unit tests for all edge cases

tasks.title: Pipeline wiring: swap beads imports to tasks
tasks.description: WHAT: Wire the new tasks.ts module into the pipeline by swapping imports in pipeline.ts, format.ts, and status.ts. After this task, forge status reads from tasks.json instead of shelling out to bd. This is the end-to-end proof that the data layer works.
tasks.design: HOW: pipeline.ts line 9: swap import { queryBeadsEpic, type EpicInfo } from './beads' to import { queryFeatureTasks, type EpicInfo } from './tasks'. Replace both queryBeadsEpic calls (lines 48, 66) with queryFeatureTasks. Leave detectPipeline and detectFeature as async functions — awaiting a sync return is harmless and avoids cascading signature changes to callers. format.ts line 7: swap import type { ReadyTask } from './beads' to './tasks'. status.ts line 11: swap import { getReadyTasks } from '../lib/beads' to '../lib/tasks'. No logic changes in any consumer — just import sources.
acceptance: pipeline.ts imports queryFeatureTasks and EpicInfo from tasks
format.ts imports ReadyTask from tasks
status.ts imports getReadyTasks from tasks
forge status displays correct stage from a manually-created tasks.json
forge status --json includes EpicInfo with epics array
forge status shows needs-tasks when no tasks.json exists
Progress bar renders correctly from tasks.json data

tasks.title: forge.json config and forge init --prefix
tasks.description: WHAT: Add project key configuration via forge.json. The readProjectPrefix function reads the prefix from forge.json and throws if missing. forge init gains a --prefix flag for non-interactive use and a readline prompt for interactive use. Existing init behavior (dirs + templates) is fully preserved.
tasks.design: HOW: Add readProjectPrefix(cwd?): string to src/lib/tasks.ts — reads forge.json from project root via readFileSync+JSON.parse, validates prefix (uppercase alphanumeric, 2-10 chars), throws 'No project key configured. Run forge init to set one.' if missing. In src/commands/init.ts: add --prefix <PREFIX> flag parsing; if TTY and no --prefix, use Node readline module for interactive prompt ('Project prefix (e.g., FORGE):') with validation loop; if non-interactive and no --prefix, skip forge.json; if forge.json exists, skip entirely (idempotent). Write { "prefix": "VALUE" } to forge.json.
acceptance: forge init --prefix FORGE creates forge.json with correct content
Prefix validation rejects: lowercase, hyphens, empty, <2 chars, >10 chars
forge init with existing forge.json skips prefix step
Non-interactive without --prefix skips forge.json creation silently
Running forge tasks after non-interactive init (no forge.json) shows clear error directing to forge init --prefix
Existing dir/template creation behavior preserved
readProjectPrefix returns valid prefix from forge.json
readProjectPrefix throws clear error when forge.json missing

tasks.title: Write operations and ID generation
tasks.description: WHAT: Implement the write-side library functions: writeTasksFile (canonical JSON formatting), createEpic (global sequential numbering), createTask (--parent resolution, nesting validation, sequential child numbering), addDep, and removeDep. These are the data-layer functions that CLI commands in FORGE-1.6 will call.
tasks.design: HOW: writeTasksFile(filePath, data: TasksFile): void — JSON.stringify with 2-space indent, schema key order (use replacer or manual ordering), trailing newline. createEpic(feature, title, cwd?): string — call discoverTaskFiles to scan all files for max epic number, increment, append Epic to file's epics array, return PREFIX-N. createTask(feature, title, parentId, opts: {priority?, labels?, description?, design?, acceptance?, notes?}, cwd?): string — validate parent exists (epic or task), validate nesting depth <= MAX_NESTING_DEPTH, find next sequential child number, append Task with defaults (status:open, deps:[], comments:[], closeReason:null). addDep(blockedId, blockerId, cwd?): void — find blocked task across files via discoverTaskFiles, append blockerId to dependencies. removeDep mirrors. All use readProjectPrefix for ID prefix.
acceptance: writeTasksFile produces 2-space-indented JSON with trailing newline

writeTasksFile preserves schema key order by constructing objects with properties in insertion order (V8/Bun preserves insertion order) — NOT via JSON.stringify replacer which does not control key order. Order: version, epics, tasks; within tasks: id, title, status, priority, labels, description, design, acceptance, notes, dependencies, comments, closeReason
writeTasksFile propagates I/O errors (disk full, permissions) as thrown exceptions
createEpic assigns globally sequential epic number across all files
createTask creates correct nested ID under parent
createTask defaults: status open, empty deps/comments/labels, null closeReason
Max 3 nesting levels enforced (error on deeper)
createTask requires parentId — errors if parent ID doesn't exist
addDep/removeDep works across files
Unit tests for ID generation and nesting validation

tasks.title: CLI subcommand dispatch and handlers
tasks.description: WHAT: Rewrite src/commands/tasks.ts as a subcommand dispatcher with handlers for: scaffold, epic create, create, dep add/remove, list, show, ready. Also update src/index.ts HELP text. This delivers the full creation and query workflow.
tasks.design: HOW: Rewrite commands/tasks.ts — parse first positional arg, check against reserved names (ready, list, show, create, close, update, comment, label, dep, validate, epic), dispatch to handler function, else treat as feature name for scaffold. Scaffold handler: check forge.json (readProjectPrefix), check plan.md exists, write empty tasks.json or show summary. --project flag sets feature context to project-level (plans/tasks.json, no plan.md check). Epic create handler: parse title from args, call createEpic. Create handler: parse --parent, --priority, --acceptance (repeated), --label (repeated), -d, --design, --notes flags, call createTask. Dep handler: parse add/remove subcommand. List/show/ready handlers: call library functions, format output. All support --json. Update index.ts HELP text: tasks line reflects subcommand model.
acceptance: forge tasks <feature> scaffolds empty tasks.json
forge tasks <feature> with existing tasks.json shows summary
forge tasks --project scaffolds plans/tasks.json
forge tasks epic create --project creates project-level epic
forge tasks create --project with --parent creates project-level task
forge tasks without forge.json shows clear error
forge tasks epic create <feature> title creates epic
forge tasks create with --parent/--acceptance/--label works
--parent defaults to sole epic when only one exists; errors when 0 or 2+ epics and not specified
Multi-epic: two epics in same feature, tasks in each, cross-epic dependency works
forge tasks dep add/remove works
forge tasks list shows tabular output grouped by epic
forge tasks show <id> displays all fields
forge tasks ready returns correct ready tasks
All commands support --json flag
index.ts HELP text updated

tasks.title: Close command with dependency validation
tasks.description: WHAT: Implement closeTask and the close CLI subcommand with the full close validation table: closed deps allowed, in_progress deps error without --force / allowed with --force, open deps always error. Stores closeReason.
tasks.design: HOW: closeTask(id, {reason?, force?}, cwd?): void — find task across files via discoverTaskFiles, read all files to check dep statuses. For each dep: if open → error naming dep (even with force); if in_progress and no force → error naming dep; if in_progress and force → allowed; if closed → allowed. Set task.status='closed', task.closeReason=reason||'completed'. Call writeTasksFile. CLI handler in commands/tasks.ts: parse --reason and --force flags, call closeTask, output result.
acceptance: Close with all deps closed succeeds
Close with in_progress dep without --force errors naming dep
Close with in_progress dep with --force succeeds
Close with open dep errors even with --force
Close with --reason stores closeReason
Close without --reason defaults to 'completed'
Unit tests for all 6 close validation cells
tasks.notes: Files: src/lib/tasks.ts (modify — add closeTask), src/commands/tasks.ts (modify — add close handler), src/lib/__tests__/tasks.test.ts (modify)

tasks.title: Auto-close parent cascade
tasks.description: WHAT: Extend closeTask to trigger auto-close of parent containers when all children are closed. Cascade upward until hitting an epic boundary (epics don't auto-close). Prevent direct close of containers with open children.
tasks.design: HOW: After closing a task in closeTask, call checkAutoClose(file, taskId). checkAutoClose: derive parentId by removing last dot-segment from numeric portion; if no dots (epic boundary) stop; find parent task; if all children of parent have status=closed, set parent.status='closed', parent.closeReason='all children closed', recurse with checkAutoClose(file, parentId). Before allowing close, check if task has children (any other task's ID starts with task.id + '.') — if so and any child is not closed, error. Auto-close happens in the same writeTasksFile call (synchronous within one write).
acceptance: Auto-close triggers when last child closes
Auto-close cascades to grandparent when all siblings closed
Auto-close stops at epic boundary (no dots in numeric portion)
Auto-close sets closeReason to 'all children closed'
Direct close of container with open/in_progress children errors
Auto-close is synchronous within same write operation
Unit tests for single-level and cascade auto-close
tasks.notes: Files: src/lib/tasks.ts (modify — extend closeTask with checkAutoClose), src/lib/__tests__/tasks.test.ts (modify)

tasks.title: Update, comment, and label mutations
tasks.description: WHAT: Implement updateTask, addComment, and addLabel library functions plus their CLI subcommand handlers. updateTask handles status changes (with closeReason clearing on revert to open), priority, title, description, design, and notes. Prevents setting container status to closed directly.
tasks.design: HOW: updateTask(id, fields: Partial<Pick<Task, 'status'|'priority'|'title'|'description'|'design'|'notes'>>, cwd?): void — find task, apply fields, if status set to 'open' clear closeReason to null, if status set to 'closed' and task has children error. addComment(id, message, cwd?): void — append {message, timestamp: new Date().toISOString()} to task.comments. addLabel(id, label, cwd?): void — append label to task.labels if not already present (idempotent). CLI handlers: update parses --status, --priority, --title, -d, --design, --notes; comment takes id and message positional; label takes id and label positional.
acceptance: update --status open clears closeReason
update --status closed on container errors
update handles --title, -d, --design, --notes, --priority
comment appends with ISO 8601 timestamp
label adds idempotently (no duplicates)
All mutations support --json output

comments.message: Sequenced after FORGE-1.8 to avoid merge conflicts on tasks.ts and tasks.test.ts — both tasks modify these files

tasks.title: DAG validation command
tasks.description: WHAT: Implement validateDag and the validate CLI subcommand. Checks: cycle detection (DFS with coloring), orphan dependency references, orphan epic references (task's epic not in epics array), and duplicate IDs across the entire project.
tasks.design: HOW: validateDag(feature: string | null, cwd?): ValidationResult ({valid, errors: Array<{type, message, ids}>}) — call discoverTaskFiles to load all project tasks. Build directed graph from dependencies arrays. Cycle detection: DFS with white/gray/black coloring; gray revisit = cycle, report involved IDs. Orphan dep refs: for each dep ID in any task, verify it exists as a task somewhere. Orphan epic refs: for each task, extract epic number from ID (first segment), verify epic exists in that file's epics array. Duplicate IDs: collect all task+epic IDs across all files, flag duplicates. CLI handler: parse feature or --project, call validateDag, format output, exit 1 if errors.
acceptance: validate detects direct cycles (A depends on B, B depends on A)
validate detects transitive cycles (A→B→C→A)
validate detects cross-file cycles
validate detects orphan dependency references
validate detects orphan epic references
validate detects duplicate IDs across files
validate exits 0 on clean DAG, exits 1 on errors
validate --json returns ValidationResult
Bulk write: manually written tasks.json passes validate
Unit tests for each validation check
tasks.notes: Files: src/lib/tasks.ts (modify — add validateDag), src/commands/tasks.ts (modify — add validate handler), src/lib/__tests__/tasks.test.ts (modify)

tasks.title: File locking with PID-based stale detection
tasks.description: WHAT: Implement advisory file locking for concurrent write safety. The lock file stores the holding process PID. Stale locks (dead PID) are automatically reclaimed. withLock provides a clean acquire-execute-release wrapper.
tasks.design: HOW: Create src/lib/lock.ts. Define lock-specific constants locally in lock.ts (LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS=5000, LOCK_RETRY_MS=100, LOCK_EXTENSION='.lock') to avoid circular dependency with tasks.ts which imports lock.ts in FORGE-1.13. acquireLock(filePath): Promise<void> — try to create filePath+LOCK_EXTENSION with PID content (writeFileSync with wx flag for atomic create). On EEXIST: read existing lock PID, check alive via process.kill(pid, 0) (catch ESRCH = dead → reclaim; EPERM = alive by another user → treat as alive). Retry every LOCK_RETRY_MS up to LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS. On timeout throw clear error. releaseLock(filePath): void — unlinkSync lock file. withLock<T>(filePath, fn: () => T | Promise<T>): Promise<T> — acquireLock, try { return await fn() } finally { releaseLock }.
acceptance: Lock creates .lock file with PID content
Second lock attempt waits and retries
Lock timeout after 5s with clear error message
Stale lock (dead PID) automatically reclaimed
EPERM from process.kill treated as alive (not stale)
withLock releases lock even on error (finally block)
Unit tests for acquire, timeout, stale recovery

tasks.title: Worktree path resolution
tasks.description: WHAT: Implement git worktree detection and resolution back to the main repo root. When running inside a worktree, .git is a file (not directory) containing a gitdir pointer. Follow it to find the main worktree's plans/ directory.
tasks.design: HOW: Create src/lib/worktree.ts. resolveRepoRoot(cwd?): string — resolve cwd to absolute. Check if .git is a file (lstatSync, isFile). If directory: return cwd (normal repo). If file: read content, parse 'gitdir: <path>' line, follow path (e.g., /main/.git/worktrees/<name>), derive main repo root by going up from .git/worktrees/<name> to the .git parent, then up one more to the repo root. Return main repo root path.
acceptance: Normal repo (.git is directory) returns cwd unchanged
Worktree (.git is file) follows gitdir path to main repo root
Works with nested worktree paths
Throws clear error if .git is missing entirely
Unit tests for main repo and worktree scenarios

tasks.title: Wire locking and worktree into tasks.ts
tasks.description: WHAT: Wrap all write operations in tasks.ts with file locking via withLock and resolve file paths through resolveRepoRoot for worktree support. This makes the task system safe for concurrent agents and worktree-based task agents. Important: wrapping synchronous write functions with async withLock changes their return types from void to Promise<void> — all callers in commands/tasks.ts must be updated to await them.
tasks.design: HOW: In tasks.ts: import withLock from lock.ts and resolveRepoRoot from worktree.ts. Add resolveTasksPath(feature, cwd?): string helper that calls resolveRepoRoot(cwd) then joins with plans/<feature>/tasks.json. Update all write functions (writeTasksFile, createEpic, createTask, closeTask, updateTask, addComment, addLabel, addDep, removeDep) to: 1) resolve file path via resolveTasksPath, 2) wrap the read-modify-write cycle with withLock(filePath, ...). This changes all write functions from sync to async (returning Promise<void>). Update all call sites in commands/tasks.ts to await these functions. Read operations (queryFeatureTasks, getReadyTasks, discoverTaskFiles) also use resolveRepoRoot for path resolution but remain synchronous (no locking needed).
acceptance: All write operations acquire lock before reading file
Lock released after write completes (even on error)
File paths resolve through worktree to main repo
Read operations use worktree resolution for path discovery
All write functions in tasks.ts are now async (return Promise<void>)
All CLI handlers in commands/tasks.ts await write function calls
Concurrent writes don't corrupt JSON (lock serializes them)
Auto-close under concurrent access: closing last child with lock held auto-closes parent atomically
Worktree integration: forge tasks update <id> --status in_progress from a worktree updates the main repo's tasks.json
tasks.notes: Files: src/lib/tasks.ts (modify), src/commands/tasks.ts (modify — await all write function calls)

tasks.title: Pipeline consumer migration and beads.ts deletion
tasks.description: WHAT: Migrate the remaining beads consumers — run.ts and reflect.ts — to import from tasks.ts. Update pipeline.ts suggestAction to return 'forge tasks ready'. Delete beads.ts. After this, forge has zero dependency on bd CLI.
tasks.design: HOW: run.ts: remove isBdAvailable import and bdAvailable check. Remove queryBeadsEpic import, add queryFeatureTasks and readProjectPrefix from tasks. Replace checks.epicId = epic.epicId with checks.epicId = epic.primaryEpicId. Replace 'no-bd' error path: try readProjectPrefix() at start, catch → exit with error. hasEpic check remains (epic !== null). reflect.ts: swap queryBeadsEpic → queryFeatureTasks import. reflect.ts only uses epic.totalTasks and epic.closedTasks which are preserved. pipeline.ts: update suggestAction 'in-progress' case from 'bd ready' to 'forge tasks ready'. Delete src/lib/beads.ts.
acceptance: run.ts imports from tasks, not beads
run.ts no longer checks isBdAvailable
run.ts uses epic.primaryEpicId for display
run.ts checks forge.json instead of bd availability
reflect.ts imports from tasks, not beads
suggestAction returns 'forge tasks ready' for in-progress
src/lib/beads.ts is deleted
TypeScript compiles with no errors
forge status, forge run, forge reflect all work without bd installed
Full pipeline integration test: create tasks → close all → forge status shows needs-reflection
Multi-epic integration test: two epics all closed → needs-reflection
Project-level tasks don't affect feature pipeline stages

tasks.title: Documentation updates: guidance, plugin, skill, readme
tasks.description: WHAT: Mechanical find-replace of all bd command references to forge tasks equivalents across 14 guidance, plugin command, skill, and readme files. Remove bd from prerequisites. Update the three-layer model to reference tasks.json instead of .beads/.
tasks.design: HOW: Find-replace patterns: bd create → forge tasks create / forge tasks epic create, bd dep add → forge tasks dep add, bd swarm validate → forge tasks validate, bd ready → forge tasks ready, bd close → forge tasks close, bd comment → forge tasks comment, bd update → forge tasks update, bd label → forge tasks label, bd list → forge tasks list, .beads/ → plans/<feature>/tasks.json, 'beads' concept → 'tasks' or 'forge tasks', remove 'bd CLI installed' from prerequisites. Files: guidance/ (tasks-process, run-process, review-gates, philosophy, docs-process, plan-process), plugin/commands/ (tasks, run, status, reflect, docs, plan), skills/forge/SKILL.md, README.md.
acceptance: No remaining 'bd ' command references in guidance/ (6 files)
No remaining 'bd ' command references in plugin/commands/ (6 files)
SKILL.md updated: description, command table, stage mapping, layer model
README.md: bd removed from prerequisites, quick start uses forge tasks
philosophy.md references tasks.json not .beads/
tasks.notes: Files: guidance/tasks-process.md, guidance/run-process.md, guidance/review-gates.md, guidance/philosophy.md, guidance/docs-process.md, guidance/plan-process.md, plugin/commands/tasks.md, plugin/commands/run.md, plugin/commands/status.md, plugin/commands/reflect.md, plugin/commands/docs.md, plugin/commands/plan.md, skills/forge/SKILL.md, README.md (all modify)
